tool.name: detect_lockfiles
tool.description:
Find all dependency lockfiles and manifests under a codebase path.
First step for SCA — shows the agent what ecosystems (npm, pip, go, maven, cargo, bundler, composer, hex, dart) are present before deciding how to scan. Recurses up to max_depth and skips the usual noise directories (node_modules, .git, .venv, vendor, dist, build, target).

parameter.name: root_path
parameter.description: Path to search. Absolute or workspace-relative. Default /workspace.

parameter.name: max_depth
parameter.description: Max directory depth to recurse. Default 5.

returns.description: root, ecosystems_detected list, lockfiles_by_ecosystem dict, total_lockfiles count.

tool.name: sca_scan_dependencies
tool.description:
Scan a codebase or container image for known-CVE dependency vulnerabilities via Trivy.
Trivy auto-detects lockfiles (package-lock.json, poetry.lock, go.sum, pom.xml, Gemfile.lock, composer.lock, Cargo.lock, etc.) and checks each package against CVE databases. Returns severity-sorted findings with CVE ID, affected package, installed/fixed versions, CVSS score, and references.
Each CRITICAL/HIGH finding should be promoted to create_vulnerability_report. Medium findings typically don't warrant a full report unless they are actively exploitable and reachable from a network-facing entry point.
scan_type: - "fs": filesystem scan of a project directory (default) - "image": container image scan (pass image name/tag in target_path)

parameter.name: target_path
parameter.description: Absolute or workspace-relative path for fs scan, or image name/tag for image scan.

parameter.name: include_dev_deps
parameter.description: Include devDependencies (npm) in the scan. Default true.

parameter.name: max_findings_per_severity
parameter.description: Cap on findings returned per severity bucket. Default 50.

returns.description: scan_type, target, total_findings, by_severity counts (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW/UNKNOWN), vulnerable_packages_count, findings bucket dict keyed by severity with full vulnerability details.
